children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Subject to the availability of appropriations under subsection (e), the Secretary, acting through the Director of the National Institute of Food and Agriculture, and working in consultation with other appropriate Federal agencies that oversee national service programs, shall administer a competitively awarded food and agriculture service learning grant program (referred to in this section as the “Program”) to increase knowledge of agriculture and improve the nutritional health of children.
to increase capacity for food, garden, and nutrition education within host organizations or entities and school cafeterias and in the classroom;
to complement and build on the efforts of the farm to school programs implemented under section 18(g) of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act ();42 U.S.C. 1769(g)

to complement efforts by the Department and school food authorities to implement the school lunch program established under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act ( et seq.) and the school breakfast program established by ;42 U.S.C. 1751section 1773 of title 42

to carry out activities that advance the nutritional health of children and nutrition education in elementary schools and secondary schools (as those terms are defined in ); andsection 7801 of title 20
to foster higher levels of community engagement and support the expansion of national service and volunteer opportunities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In carrying out the Program, the Director of the National Institute of Food and Agriculture shall make competitive grants to eligible entities that carry out the purposes described in paragraphs (1) through (5) of subsection (b).

children.label: A
children.text: have a proven track record in carrying out the purposes described in subsection (b);

children.label: B
children.text: work in underserved rural and urban communities;

children.label: C
children.text: teach and engage children in experiential learning about agriculture, gardening, nutrition, cooking, and where food comes from; and

children.label: D
children.text: facilitate a connection between elementary schools and secondary schools and agricultural producers in the local and regional area.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may require a partner organization or other qualified entity to collect and report any data on the activities carried out under the Program, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: conduct regular evaluations of the activities carried out under the Program; and

children.label: B
children.text: submit to the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate a report that includes a description of the results of each evaluation conducted under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 1 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out the Program $25,000,000, to remain available until expended.

children.heading: Administration
children.label: 2 Administration
children.text: Paragraphs (4), (7), (8), and (11)(B) of subsection (b) of shall apply with respect to the making of a competitive grant under this section.section 3157 of this title

children.heading: Maintenance of effort
children.label: 3 Maintenance of effort
children.text: Funds made available under paragraph (1) shall be used only to supplement, not to supplant, the amount of Federal funding otherwise expended for nutrition, research, and extension programs of the Department.
